description_long: This is a tall, slender stone obelisk with a rectangular cross-section, rising vertically from a broad, stepped base. The obelisk is constructed from light gray stone, showing signs of weathering and some discoloration, likely from age and exposure to the elements. The base is wider than the shaft and features a simple, blocky design with no visible decorative carvings or embellishments. The tip of the obelisk is pointed, forming a subtle pyramid shape, and the entire structure stands on a patch of ground partially covered with snow and scattered green vegetation. The surface of the stone appears rough and matte, with some dirt and moss accumulating at the base. There are no visible inscriptions or ornamentation on the shaft or base, giving the monument a solemn and unadorned appearance.
description: A tall, weathered gray stone obelisk with a pointed tip and a broad, stepped base, standing on snowy ground with some vegetation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the obelisk, its pointed tip, and the broad, stepped base with snow and vegetation at the bottom.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slender profile of the obelisk and the thickness of the base, with snow and plants visible around the foundation.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back face of the obelisk and the symmetrical, blocky base, with more snow coverage and some scattered leaves.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the narrowness of the shaft and the accumulation of vegetation at the base.
attribute_base_design: The base is rectangular and stepped, constructed from the same gray stone as the shaft. It is blocky and unadorned, with some dirt and moss at the edges, and sits flush with the snowy ground.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is pointed, forming a subtle pyramid shape, with no additional decoration or unique features.
attribute_surrounding_features: The obelisk is surrounded by a patch of snow-covered ground with scattered green vegetation and some exposed soil.
